title: ferray-core: expose RawArrayView for negative-stride view construction
description: Follow-up from #737. ArrayView::from_shape_ptr in ferray-core/src/array/view.rs uses ndarray::ArrayView::from_shape_ptr which validates strides as non-negative. Negative-stride views (numpy a[::-1]-style) need the RawArrayView path: ndarray::RawArrayView::from_shape_ptr (no stride validation) followed by .deref_into_view() inside an unsafe block. Add a new ferray-core constructor (e.g. ArrayView::from_shape_ptr_signed) that wraps that path. Once this lands, ferray-stride-tricks::as_strided_signed can drop its negative-stride rejection and properly support reversed views.
